finding_results.notes: Rebuilt the settlement arrival around the crack, retaining wall, and well. Removed the multi-observer inventory and kept only a single local read of Kem through his voice.
Journal beats now enter through route certainty, fresh corrections, and the usable window of the road instead of re-describing contrasting scripts or two handwritings.
Cut the narrator-summary of Dren's baseline fear for Mira and replaced it with a brief physical check of his damaged hand before he reports on whether it is still useful.

Compressed the secondary wall-labor block into a short consequence passage, front-loaded the public level arithmetic, and made the back half of the chapter center on explicit route/level terms and Kem's decision to stay in motion.

summary: Chapter 14 now enters through settlement-specific geography, keeps the collapsing-house rescue as the opening ordeal, compresses the repeated infrastructure-labor beat, and lets the chapter's dominant second movement become witness pressure, signet shame, explicit travel terms, and Kem's choice to keep going.
